demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse, who is present and made the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I feel like I'm going to choke every time I take a bite; the food just sits in the back of my throat and won't go down.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Spouse called during dinner — she got very frightened and distressed with a piece of chicken feeling stuck in her throat, kept coughing and clearing her throat, and they wanted to be sure it wasn't something dangerous before morning.

symptom_narrative.onset: gradually over the last six months or so, slowly getting harder
symptom_narrative.duration: months, worse in the last few weeks
symptom_narrative.character: a feeling of food sticking at the back of the throat; she has to swallow several times to get a single mouthful down
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: frightening tonight with the dry chicken, but day to day it is a slow, tiring struggle rather than a sudden blockage
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: high up, at the back of the throat
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: with almost every meal, worst with dry or tough foods
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: soft, moist foods; taking tiny bites; going very slowly
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: dry or tough foods like steak or chicken breast, and eating quickly
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: frequent throat clearing and a bit of coughing during or just after eating; meals now take her over an hour and leave her exhausted
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no single choking-on-a-bone event before; it has been a slow change over months
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: meals take more than an hour, she is worn out by them, she has started avoiding eating with the family because she is embarrassed and frightened of choking

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: generally frail and slowing down with age; no specific throat or stomach condition that she knows of

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular tablets that she can think of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with her spouse who does the cooking; she has stopped joining family meals because eating has become so stressful

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says she has lost about 4 kilos over the last three months and is eating a lot less than she used to
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: estimated by spouse, not a measured serial weight

objective_data_offered.value: sitting upright at the dining table, repeatedly clearing her throat, but speaking in full sentences and breathing quietly without any harsh or noisy breathing
she can still swallow her own spit without coughing and is not drooling
a large piece of dry, tough chicken breast on the dinner plate that set the episode off tonight
